_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: .mypy_cache triggers false-positive gitleaks findings (S12 -> S16 ordering bug)

steps.description: Append .mypy_cache/, .ruff_cache/, .pytest_cache/ to [allowlist].paths in .gitleaks.toml. Add inline comment citing I-00085 next to existing __pycache__/ entry.
Per-agent review of S01: regex pattern matches existing style, no over-broad allowlist entries, comment cites I-00085.
Author tests/integration/test_security_secrets_cache_independence.py — reproduction test (type-check then security-secrets is clean) + negative test (real secret in fixture still detected).
Per-agent review of S03 tests: semantic assertions, both positive and negative paths, global-state mutation handled cleanly.
Cross-agent final review of S01..S04: scope discipline, allowlist not over-broad, real-secret detection unchanged.
QV: Secret scan (this very gate; must run clean for the first time without `rm -rf .mypy_cache` operator workaround)
Self-assessment via iw-item-analyze — focus on whether S13 (security-secrets) of this very CR ran clean on first try (it MUST, given the fix is now active).
